name: Dicaeidae
description: Flowerpeckers are a family of tiny, active passerine birds found in South Asia, Southeast Asia, and Australasia. They feed primarily on fruits, especially mistletoe berries, and play an important role in seed dispersal.

children.name: Dicaeum
children.description: The largest genus of flowerpeckers, with over 40 species of tiny, short-tailed birds found across Asia and Australasia.

children.name: Dicaeum cruentatum
children.description: The Scarlet-backed Flowerpecker is a tiny bird with brilliant red upperparts in males and grey-white underparts. It is common in gardens and forests across South and Southeast Asia, feeding on mistletoe berries.

children.name: Dicaeum trigonostigma
children.description: The Orange-bellied Flowerpecker has blue-grey upperparts and a bright orange belly and throat in males. It inhabits lowland forests and mangroves across Southeast Asia and parts of India.

children.name: Dicaeum minullum
children.description: The Plain Flowerpecker is a drab olive-grey bird with pale underparts, lacking the bright colors of most flowerpeckers. It forages in the canopy of forests and plantations across Sri Lanka, India, and Southeast Asia.

children.name: Dicaeum hirundinaceum
children.description: The Mistletoebird is a small flowerpecker with glossy blue-black upperparts and a red chest and belly in males. It is found across Australia, specializing on mistletoe berries which it disperses through its rapid digestive system.

children.name: Dicaeum australe
children.description: A small flowerpecker with olive-brown upperparts, a red patch on the lower back, and white underparts with red streaking on the breast, endemic to the Philippines. It inhabits lowland forests, second growth, and gardens, feeding on mistletoe berries.

children.name: Dicaeum chrysorrheum
children.description: A small flowerpecker with olive-green upperparts, pale underparts with dark streaking, and a bright yellow vent, found in forests of the Himalayas, Southeast Asia, Sumatra, and Borneo. It forages actively in the canopy, often in mixed-species flocks.

children.name: Dicaeum agile
children.description: A dull-coloured flowerpecker with a relatively thick, heavy bill, olive-brown upperparts, and pale underparts with diffuse streaking, found across South and Southeast Asia. It inhabits forests, plantations, and gardens, feeding on mistletoe berries and small fruits.

children.name: Dicaeum aureolimbatum
children.description: A small flowerpecker endemic to Sulawesi and neighbouring Indonesian islands, with olive-green upperparts, a yellow stripe on the side of the breast, and white underparts. It inhabits lowland and montane forests, feeding on mistletoe berries and small fruits.

children.name: Dicaeum celebicum
children.description: A small flowerpecker with grey upperparts, a white throat, and pale underparts, endemic to Sulawesi and adjacent islands. It inhabits lowland and montane forests, often seen in the canopy feeding on mistletoe berries. Its grey sides distinguish it from other Sulawesi flowerpeckers.

children.name: Dicaeum ignipectus
children.description: A tiny, colourful flowerpecker with glossy blue-black upperparts, a brilliant red patch on the breast, and buff underparts, found from the Himalayas through Southeast Asia to Borneo and the Philippines. It is one of the most striking flowerpeckers, the male's fiery red breast patch giving it its name.

children.name: Dicaeum monticolum
children.description: A small, distinctive flowerpecker endemic to the mountains of Borneo, with blue-black upperparts, a red throat and breast, and black sides contrasting with a white belly. It inhabits montane forests above 1,000 metres, feeding on mistletoe berries.
